The Lekkie Drive Cover 01/02 has a gasket
already assmebled on on the the cover. These
When installing the Lekkie Drive Cover 01/02 you will require some tools and some hands on experience
your local bike shop or engeering shop and they should be able to help you out.
Here are some tips to keep in mind while completing the install.
•� To keep everything running smooth end easy to maintain make sure you grease all metal to metal
•� Clean any excess grease out of the motor while it is open.
•� Clean and grease the main gear and pinion.
•� Always grease the axle taper before install cranks.
LEKKIE BLING RING 40T 01/02 USER GUIDE V4.2 WWW.LEKKIE.TECH
1. Unscrew the screws holding on the stock cover and remove.
2. Clean and lubricate the main drive gear.
3. Clean the mounting surface of the motor body.
4. Wipe a smudge of grease onto the inside edge of the rubber lip of the Lekkie Drive Cover.
5. Slide the Lekkie Drive Cover onto the motor.
6. Apply a drop of Loctite 243 to each screw and screw them in. Tighten to 3Nm.
To keep your motor running its best its important to regularly grease the main drive gear and pinion.
We have added a grease port to the Lekkie Drive Cover 01/02 to allow this.
To do so simply use a grease gun to squirt moly-grease in while slowly turning the motor.
The port is postitioned at the junction of the drive gear and pinion.
LEKKIE BLING RING 40T 01/02 USER GUIDE V4.2 WWW.LEKKIE.TECH
•� Clean and lubricate axle with a multi-pupose grease before install.
•� Always install a new chain when installing a Bling Ring
•� If you are offsetting by more than 2mm use longer bolts.
•� Make sure when you install your Bling Ring you have at least 1.5mm gap between wide tooth and
•� Try to get the chainline to the centre of your cassette - see below for details.
•� DO NOT use half links in the chain.
•� Always grease axle before re-installing cranks.
LEKKIE BLING RING 40T 01/02 USER GUIDE V4.2 WWW.LEKKIE.TECH
Here are the basic dimensions of the Bling Ring. Note the image is just an illsutation to explain the
Note the package includes a 2mm Spacer so that you can space the Bling Ring out to the same offset as
the original Bling Ring offset. We decided to offset the Bling Ring an extra 2mm so that useres could try
get that perfect chainline for the 73mm BB frames. If you are building a 73mm BB frame try installing
without the spacer. You should have at least 1.5mm gap between the ring and chain-stay.
LEKKIE BLING RING 40T 01/02 USER GUIDE V4.2 WWW.LEKKIE.TECH
When building your machine you want to replicate the chainline that was on the bike before you converted
it. Ideally you want the chainline to line up with the centre of your cassette.
The Blingring is designed to give you the best possible chainline however sometimes you may need to
adjust your offset using spacers to get that chainline perfectly suited to the unique geometry of your build.
The Lekkie Bling Ring is compatible with most chains up to 11-speed (known to work with some 12 speeds
too). If your chain is not sitting well on the chainring try using a Shimano chain compatible with the
number of gears. EG HG-X11 11 Speed. Note if the chain is tight on the teeth do not use.
The 42T and 46T offset gives you the best possible chainline without interfering with the motor. However
chainstay interference is a big issue in building your bike - the larger the sprocket the closer the chainstay
gets so you may need to use a spacer to clear the chainstay.

At Lekkie we don’t accept anything but the best workmanship and quality. If a part has slipped through our
QC system and made it to your hands let us know and we will put it right.
If one of our products fails we want to know so we can make our products better. If you destroy a lekkie
product in extreme use tell us. We can learn from this and sell you something so suit your needs.
If you are in anyway unhappy with your Lekkie product get in touch so we can put it right.
